Items.Description:
一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 devteam 的操作：

降低 devteam 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning devteam 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, devteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag devteam:

Make all posts by devteam less visible

devteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: thepracticaldev

Items.Title: 指令和依赖注入的超能力：第 6 部分 | Superpowers with Directives and Dependency Injection: Part 6
Items.Description:
该是关于 Angular 指令及其近乎神奇的超能力的系列文章的第 6 部分了。
今天我们将探索一个不太流行但非常非常普遍且有效的用例场景——将业务逻辑引入指令中。

介绍

首先，让我们反思一下我们已经做了什么，并了解今天的案例有何根本不同。

在之前的文章中，我们创建了指令来完成一些特定的、可重用的、非常有用的事情。

例如，我们构建了一个指令

  • 检查密码强度
  • 当宿主元素滚动到视图中时发出自定义事件
  • 将模板视图传输到另一个组件
  • 显示一个装载机
  • 在现有组件上添加了一些默认输入

如您所见，所有这些情况都有一个共同的主题——所有这些指令都是超级可重用的，因为它们可以放在任何项目中并且可以正常工作； 它们中没有一个真正包含任何业务逻辑。 但在很多情况下，对于现实生活中的项目，我们希望可重用和易于共享的是业务逻辑。 那么有哪些场景呢？

使用指令处理权限

想象一下，我们的应用程序有权启用或禁用某些功能。 例如，我们可能有一项功能允许用户编辑他们的个人资料，但前提是他们拥有正确的权限。 我们有一些PermissionService有一个hasPermission方法，它接受一个权限名称并返回一个布尔值。 我们可以像这样在我们的组件中使用这个服务：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *ngIf="hasPermission('edit-profile')"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { constructor(private permissionService: PermissionService) {} hasPermission(permissionName: string) { return this.permissionService.hasPermission(permissionName); } editProfile() { // ... } }

但是现在每次我们想要检查这个或那个权限时，我们都需要注入服务，也许编写一个包装方法，从模板中调用它，或者其他一些稍微麻烦的方式。 此外，如果我们访问这些权限的方式发生变化（例如，服务现在具有不同的 API 接口），我们可能会面临相当大的重构。 此外，如果我们有某种与 Observables 一起工作的状态管理解决方案（例如 NgRx），我们的代码将变得更加复杂。

那么我们如何处理呢？

好吧，如果我们可以这样做，那不是很好吗：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *hasPermission="'edit-profile'"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { editProfile() { // ... } }

所以现在我们可以将权限名称作为输入传递给指令，指令将处理其余部分。 让我们看看如何做到这一点。

  • 首先，我们需要创建一个注入PermissionService的指令，并具有一个接受权限名称的输入属性
  • 接下来，我们将添加ngIf作为 HostDirective，以便它可以处理显示/隐藏模板
  • 最后，我们将注入对NgIf指令的引用，并将结果布尔值传递给它

我们开工吧：

 @Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf], }) export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { // we can use any other approach here this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } }

现在，我们的指令几乎可以完美运行。 我们需要添加什么？ 当然，也可以处理用户没有权限的情况。 我们可以通过在我们的指令中添加一个else模板来做到这一点：

 @Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf], }) export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } // the improtant part @Input() set hasPermissionElse(template: TemplateRef<any>) { this.ngIfRef.ngIfElse = template; } }

现在，我们基本上只是执行业务逻辑并将结果传递给NgIf指令。 这是一个带有预览的工作示例：

使用指令处理组件中的共享数据

想象一下，我们使用一些 UI 库来显示下拉菜单：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="dropdownItems"></third-party-dropdown> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { dropdownItems = [ { label: 'Item 1', value: 1 }, { label: 'Item 2', value: 2 } ]; }

在某些情况下，我们需要将相同的选项列表传递给多个下拉列表。 例如，我们在本文前面遇到的相同权限列表在很多地方可能非常有用：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></third-party-dropdown> </div> <div> ` }) export class SomeComponent { permissionService = inject(PermissionService); permissions = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); }

然后我们真的可以在整个应用程序的多个地方看到相同的代码。 那么我们怎样才能使它更具可重用性呢？ 好吧，我们可以创建一个组件来包装下拉列表并接受项目列表作为输入：

 <app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown>

但这会带来一系列问题：

  • 最终必须向上和向下传递所有第三方下拉菜单的输入和输出
  • 我们的模板更加复杂

那么我们可以做什么呢？ 好吧，我们可以创建一个指令，将项目列表注入第三方下拉列表：

 @Directive({ selector: 'third-party-dropdown[permissionList]', standalone: true, }) export class PermissionsDropdownDirective implements OnInit { thirdPartyDropdown = inject(ThirdPartyDropdown); permissionService = inject(PermissionService); ngOnInit() { this.thirdPartyDropdown.items = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); } }

现在我们可以像这样使用它：

 <third-party-dropdown permissionList></third-party-dropdown>

就是这样！ 现在我们的模板很简单， third-party-dropdown仍然和以前一样，我们还为它增加了可重用的功能。

这是带有预览的示例：

结论

我们已经探索了指令给我们的大量可能性。 虽然还有很多，但对于不同规模的项目来说这些已经足够了，所以本系列文章即将结束。 在下一篇和最后一篇中，我们将详细讨论指令选择器，我们可以使用什么，我们如何组合不同的选择器，以及有哪些陷阱或我们不能使用某个选择器的地方。 敬请关注！



原文:

Original cover photo by Ivan Bandura on Unsplash.

It's time for the 6th installment of this article series about Angular directives - and their almost magical superpowers.
Today we are going to explore a less popular, but very, very common and valid use case scenario - bringing business logic into the directives.

Introduction

First of all, let's reflect on what we have already done, and understand how today's cases are fundamentally different.

In previous articles, we created directives that did some specific, reusable, and very useful things.

For example, we built a directive that

  • checked for password strength
  • emitted custom events when the host element scrolled into view
  • transported a template view into another component
  • displayed a loader
  • added some default inputs onto existing components

As you can see, there is a common theme for all these cases - all those directives are super-reusable in the sense that they can be dropped in any project and just work; non of them really contain any business logic. But in a lot of cases, with real-life projects, it is the business logic that we want to be reusable and easily shareable. So what scenarios are there?

Using directives to handle permissions

Imagine that our app has permissions to enable or disable certain features. For example, we might have a feature that allows users to edit their profile, but only if they have the right permissions. We have a few PermissionService that has a hasPermission method that takes a permission name and returns a boolean. We could use this service in our component like this:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *ngIf="hasPermission('edit-profile')"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { constructor(private permissionService: PermissionService) {} hasPermission(permissionName: string) { return this.permissionService.hasPermission(permissionName); } editProfile() { // ... }
}

But now every time we want to check for this or that permission, we need to inject the service, maybe write a wrapper method, call it from the template, or some other mildly cumbersome way. Also, if the way how we access those permissions changes (for example, the service now has a different API interface), we might face a pretty large refactoring. Also, if were have some sort of state management solution that works with Observables (like NgRx, for instance), our code will become even more complex.

So how do we deal with this?

Well, wouldn't it be nice if we could just do this:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *hasPermission="'edit-profile'"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { editProfile() { // ... }
}

So now we can pass the permission name as an input to the directive, and the directive will handle the rest. Let's see how we can do this.

  • First, we need to create a directive that injects the PermissionService and has an input property that will accept the permission name
  • next, we will add ngIf as a HostDirective so it can handle showing/hiding the template
  • and finally, we will inject the reference to the NgIf directive, and pass the resulting boolean to it

Let's do this:

@Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf],
})
export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { // we can use any other approach here this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); }
}

Now, our directive works almost perfectly. What do we need to add? Of course, a possibility to handle the case when the user does not have the permission. We can do this by adding an else template to our directive:

@Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf],
})
export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } // the improtant part @Input() set hasPermissionElse(template: TemplateRef<any>) { this.ngIfRef.ngIfElse = template; }
}

Now, we essentially just do the business logic and pass the result to the NgIf directive. Here is a working example with a preview:

Using directives to handle shared data in components

Imagine we use some UI library that allows us to show dropdowns:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="dropdownItems"></third-party-dropdown> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { dropdownItems = [ { label: 'Item 1', value: 1 }, { label: 'Item 2', value: 2 } ]; }

And in some cases, we need to pass the same list of options to multiple dropdowns. For example, the same list of permissions we encountered earlier in this article could be really useful in lots of places:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></third-party-dropdown> </div> <div> `
})
export class SomeComponent { permissionService = inject(PermissionService); permissions = this.permissionService.getPermissions();
}

And then we could really see this same code in multiple places throughout our app. So how can we make this more reusable? Well, we could create a component that will wrap the dropdown and accept the list of items as an input:

<app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown>

But that would introduce a set of problems:

  • CSS encapsulation
  • Having to pass eventually all of the third-party dropdown's inputs and outputs up and down
  • More complexity in our templates

So what can we do instead? Well, we can create a directive that will inject the list of items into the third-party dropdown:

@Directive({ selector: 'third-party-dropdown[permissionList]', standalone: true,
})
export class PermissionsDropdownDirective implements OnInit { thirdPartyDropdown = inject(ThirdPartyDropdown); permissionService = inject(PermissionService); ngOnInit() { this.thirdPartyDropdown.items = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); }
}

And now we can use it like this:

<third-party-dropdown permissionList></third-party-dropdown>

And that's it! Now our template is simple, the third-party-dropdown is still as it used to be, and we got additional reusable functionality for it.

Here is the example with a preview:

Conclusion

We have explored a huge amount of possibilities that directives give us. While there are plenty more, this much is fairly enough for projects of different sizes, so this article series is nearing its end. In the next and last one, we will talk in detail about directive selectors, what we can use, how can we combine different selectors, and what are some pitfalls or where we cannot use a certain selector. Stay tuned!
Items.Author: armandotrue

Items.Title: DevOps 服务在现代软件开发中的作用 | The Role of DevOps Services in Modern Software Development
Items.Description:
该方法旨在将软件开发和运营相结合，以提高效率并提高软件交付的质量。 通过将DevOps 服务集成到您的软件开发流程中，您可以获得多项好处，包括更快的上市时间、增强开发和运营团队之间的协作、提高软件质量以及提高自动化程度。

什么是 DevOps 服务？

它们涉及一种协作方法，旨在消除孤岛并简化软件交付过程。 更快的上市时间： DevOps 服务的主要优势之一是更快的上市时间。 通过将持续集成和部署 (CI/CD) 管道集成到您的软件开发流程中，您可以自动化代码的构建、测试和部署，从而实现更快、更频繁的发布。 这使您能够快速响应不断变化的客户需求和市场条件，从而获得竞争优势。

加强协作： DevOps 服务还促进开发和运营团队之间加强协作。 通过打破孤岛和鼓励跨职能团队，您可以改善沟通和协作，从而提高软件质量和效率。

提高软件质量： DevOps 服务在提高软件质量方面也发挥着关键作用。 通过将测试和监控工具集成到您的软件开发流程中，您可以在开发周期的早期识别和修复问题，从而降低生产中出现错误和错误的风险。 这导致更好的软件质量和更高的客户满意度。

提高自动化程度： DevOps 服务还可以提高软件开发过程的自动化程度。 通过自动化重复性和手动任务，例如测试和部署，您可以降低出错风险并提高效率。 这可以让您的开发团队腾出时间专注于更高价值的任务，例如开发新特性和功能。

更好的监控：最后，DevOps 服务可以更好地监控您的软件和基础设施。 通过将监控工具集成到您的软件开发流程中，您可以实时了解应用程序的性能和行为，从而在问题影响您的客户之前快速识别并解决问题。

实施 DevOps 服务可能具有挑战性，但 CloudZenix 可以帮助您取得成功。 我们提供一系列 DevOps 服务，包括持续集成和部署 (CI/CD)、基础设施即代码 (IaC)、自动化和监控，以帮助您简化软件开发流程并实现您的业务目标。

我们的 DevOps 专家团队可以与您一起设计和实施针对您的特定需求量身定制的 DevOps 策略，同时考虑您现有的基础设施、开发方法和业务目标。 我们可以帮助您实施自动化测试和部署管道、改进基础设施管理，并将监控和分析工具集成到您的软件开发过程中。

除了我们的 DevOps 服务，我们还提供一系列其他云服务，包括云迁移、云优化和云安全，以帮助您充分利用云的强大功能来开展业务。

结论

通过在您的软件开发流程中实施 DevOps 服务，您可以获得竞争优势并实现您的业务目标。 立即联系 CloudZenix，详细了解我们如何帮助您实施 DevOps 服务并在云中取得成功。 cloudzenix.com/contact-us/ cloudzenix.com/contact-us/



原文:


DevOps services play a critical role in modern software development. The approach aims to combine software development and operations to increase efficiency and improve the quality of software delivery. By integrating DevOps services into your software development process, you can gain several benefits, including faster time-to-market, increased collaboration between development and operations teams, improved software quality, and increased automation.

What are DevOps Services?

DevOps services refer to the processes, tools, and methodologies that facilitate the integration of software development and operations. They involve a collaborative approach that aims to eliminate silos and streamline the software delivery process. DevOps services typically include continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD), infrastructure as code (IaC), automation, and monitoring.

The Role of DevOps Services in Modern Software Development

Faster Time-to-Market: One of the primary benefits of DevOps services is faster time-to-market. By integrating continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) pipelines into your software development process, you can automate the build, test, and deployment of code, enabling faster and more frequent releases. This allows you to respond quickly to changing customer needs and market conditions, giving you a competitive advantage.

Increased Collaboration: DevOps services also promote increased collaboration between development and operations teams. By breaking down silos and encouraging cross-functional teams, you can improve communication and collaboration, resulting in better software quality and increased efficiency.

Improved Software Quality: DevOps services also play a critical role in improving software quality. By integrating testing and monitoring tools into your software development process, you can identify and fix issues earlier in the development cycle, reducing the risk of bugs and errors in production. This leads to better software quality and improved customer satisfaction.

Increased Automation: DevOps services also enable increased automation in your software development process. By automating repetitive and manual tasks, such as testing and deployment, you can reduce the risk of errors and increase efficiency. This frees up your development team to focus on higher-value tasks, such as developing new features and functionality.

Better Monitoring: Finally, DevOps services enable better monitoring of your software and infrastructure. By integrating monitoring tools into your software development process, you can gain real-time insights into the performance and behavior of your applications, allowing you to quickly identify and resolve issues before they impact your customers.

How CloudZenix Can Help You Implement DevOps Services

Implementing DevOps services can be challenging, but CloudZenix can help you achieve success. We offer a range of DevOps services, including continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD), infrastructure as code (IaC), automation, and monitoring, to help you streamline your software development process and achieve your business objectives.

Our team of DevOps experts can work with you to design and implement a DevOps strategy tailored to your specific needs, taking into account your existing infrastructure, development methodologies, and business goals. We can help you implement automated testing and deployment pipelines, improve infrastructure management, and integrate monitoring and analytics tools into your software development process.

In addition to our DevOps services, we also offer a range of other cloud services, including cloud migration, cloud optimization, and cloud security, to help you leverage the full power of the cloud for your business.

Conclusion

DevOps services play a critical role in modern software development, enabling faster time-to-market, increased collaboration, improved software quality, increased automation, and better monitoring. By implementing DevOps services into your software development process, you can gain a competitive advantage and achieve your business objectives. Contact CloudZenix today to learn more about how we can help you implement DevOps services and achieve success in the cloud. cloudzenix.com/contact-us/
